body: In the esbuild fallback path, the bundle is written to `<outDir>/<plugin>.min.js`, but profiles/external_plugins URLs in this repo point to `<outDir>/plugin.min.js` (see assets/generated/profiles.js). If a plugin build fails or a plugin has no prebuilt dist, TinyMCE will try to load plugin.min.js and fail. Align the fallback output naming with the expected `plugin.min.js` (and, if needed, also emit an unminified `plugin.js`) or adjust the external_plugins URL generation consistently.

`imageswappool` delegates to `editor.execCommand('mceImage')`, but that command is only available when TinyMCE's built-in `image` plugin is enabled. Since profiles can toggle plugins independently, this button can become a no-op (or error) if `image` is disabled while `for_images` is enabled. Consider guarding via `editor.queryCommandSupported('mceImage')`/`editor.plugins.image` and showing a clear notification when the image dialog isn't available.

The esbuild fallback bundles without `external: ['tinymce']`. Since plugin sources import from `tinymce`, this fallback will likely inline the entire TinyMCE package into each plugin bundle (large output and potential runtime duplication), whereas the per-plugin build scripts externalize tinymce. Add `external: ['tinymce']` (and keep the global `tinymce` runtime dependency) to make the fallback behave consistently with the normal plugin builds.

When running with `--staging`, this script still executes each plugin’s `pnpm run build` (which typically runs `build-copy` and writes directly into `assets/scripts/...`). That undermines the purpose of a staging build (`build/` should be the only output of `build:staging`) and can leave the working tree dirty or with stale files if `build-copy` doesn’t fully overwrite/remove previous outputs. Consider skipping plugin `build-copy` in staging mode (e.g. run only the build step that produces `dist/`, or pass an env/flag that makes plugin builds write into `build/plugins/` instead of `assets/`).
